findings.issue: verify field embeds the command inline in the value: `verify: bash — `go test ./cmd/report/...``. Template (and tasks 01/02/04/05) put the tool name as the value (`verify: bash`) and the command in a trailing `# comment`. Here the value is `bash — go test ...`, which is no longer a clean tool name.
findings.fix: Set `verify: bash` and move the command into a comment: `verify: bash                       # go test ./cmd/report/...`, matching tasks 01 and 02.

findings.issue: Project-knowledge link uses mismatched text and path: `[project.md](.claude/skills/project-knowledge/overview.md)` — link label says project.md but target is overview.md. The repo has no project.md (overview.md is the analog). Tasks 01/02/04/05 correctly label this link `overview.md`.
findings.fix: Rename the link text to `overview.md` to match the actual target (as in the other tasks): `[overview.md](.claude/skills/project-knowledge/overview.md)`.

findings.issue: Feature-artifact, reviewer-report and decisions links use full repo-relative paths (docs/features/008-.../008-...) while tasks 01/02/04 use bare basenames (008-...md). Links resolve either way, but the batch is inconsistent in path style.
findings.fix: Pick one convention across the batch. Since these files sit in the feature dir alongside the task file, prefer the bare-basename style used by tasks 01/02/04.

findings.issue: Same path-style inconsistency as task 03: full repo-relative paths for feature artifacts and reviewer reports, whereas tasks 01/02/04 use bare basenames.
findings.fix: Normalize link paths to the bare-basename style used by tasks 01/02/04 for consistency across the batch.

findings.issue: Mixed relative-path styles within the same task: Project-knowledge and some code-file links use ../../../ ascent (e.g. [report/report_test.go](../../../report/report_test.go)) while feature-artifact links use bare basenames. Both resolve, but the ascent form differs from tasks 01/02 which use plain repo-relative paths (report/...).
findings.fix: Use one path convention. Plain repo-relative paths (report/..., .claude/...) as in tasks 01/02 are clearer than ../../../ ascent.
